sales.title:
AEOLIS. Temnus. Ca. 200-170 BC. AR tetradrachm (36mm, 15.94 gm, 1h). NGC AU 5/5 - 3/5. Late posthumous issue in the name and types of Alexander III the Great of Macedon. Head of Heracles right, wearing lion skin headdress, paws tied before neck / AΛEΞANΔPOY, Zeus enthroned left, right leg drawn back, feet on groundline, eagle in outstretched right hand, grounded scepter in left; HAT(?) monogram and ΣΩ monogram above oenochoe within vine tendril in left field. Price 1689. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
Ancients

LESBOS. Mytilene. Ca. 188-170 BC. AR tetradrachm (35mm, 17.11 gm, 11h). NGC XF 4/5 - 4/5, Fine Style, edge bend, brushed. Late posthumous issue in the name and types of Alexander III the Great of Macedon, ca. 188-170 BC. Head of Heracles right, wearing lion skin headdress, paws tied before neck / AΛEΞANΔPOY, Zeus seated left on backless throne, right leg drawn back, feet on stool, eagle in outstretched right hand, grounded scepter in left; AT monogram above lyre in left field, ivy leaf below throne. Price 1736. Reflective, cherry blossom toning. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
Ancients

PAMPHYLIA. Aspendus. Ca. 212-181 BC. AR tetradrachm (30mm, 16.76 gm, 1h). NGC AU 5/5 - 3/5, Fine Style, brushed. Late posthumous issue in the name and types of Alexander III the Great of Macedon, dated Civic Year 11 (ca. 202/201 BC(?)). Head of Heracles right, wearing lion skin headdress, paws tied before neck / AΛEΞANΔPOY, Zeus seated left on backless throne, right leg drawn back, feet on ground line, eagle in outstretched right hand, grounded scepter in left; AΣ above IA (date) in left field. Price 2891. Boldy rendered details with mirror-like luster. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
Ancients

IONIA. Smyrna. Ca. late 3rd century BC. AR tetradrachm (28mm, 17.06 gm, 1h). NGC AU 5/5 - 3/5, Fine Style, brushed. Late posthumous issue in the name and types of Alexander III the Great of Macedon, ca. 220-200 BC. Head of Heracles right, wearing lion skin headdress, paws tied before neck / ΑΛΕΞΑΝΔΡΟΥ, Zeus enthroned left, right leg drawn back, feet on ground line, eagle in outstretched right hand, grounded scepter in left; turreted head of Tyche right above ΠXAE monogram in left field. Price 2248. Seemingly no records in sales archives. Beautifully rendered. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
Ancients

THRACE. Odessus. Ca. 125-70 BC. AR tetradrachm (30mm, 1h). NGC XF, slight die shift. Late posthumous issue in the name and types of Alexander III the Great of Macedon. Head of Heracles right, wearing lion-skin headdress, paws tied before neck / AΛEΞANΔPOY / BAΣIΛEΩΣ, Zeus seated on backless throne left, right leg drawn back, feet on ground line, eagle in outstretched right hand, grounded scepter in left; OΔH in inner left field, HPAKΛEOYΣ in exergue. Price 1208 var. (HPAKΛEOY). Only one other example of this variation found in sales archives. From The Salvager Collection. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
